pages.raw_text:
Injunction proceedings will likely
be started against the Walters and
Cooks and Kitchen Helpers unions
by the Cafaterias and Baltimore
lunch coutners unless the pickets
are withdrawn from in front Of
these places. One arrest was made
yesterday afternoon in front of the
New Cafeteria, Sprague avenue
near Washington street, but as no
warrant was sworn out the picket
was released.


Pickets are stationed in front OF
nearly every one of these places to
day, shouting to the patrons that
the places are unfair to organized
labor. 1t is officially stated that
they will be kept at work until they
are restrained by law, or until the
Baltimore Lunches and Cafaterias
consent to unionize their help. Pro.
prietors of these places are becom-
ing hostile.


Unionization is practically impoa-
sible, declare some of the proprie-
tors of the eating places. In only
one Cafeteria men cooks are hired.
Female help is employed almost ex-
clusively In all others. Fixe extra
boys help about an hour or two at
lunch time each day for their board
and the proprietors declare they
cannot afford to pay them the union
scale.
